The '''Second Battle of Zonama Sekot''' was a battle in [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]] which took place at [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]], a "living planet" located within the [[Gardaji Rift/Legends|Gardaji Rift]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="RP">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref>

==Background==
Prior to the battle, Zonama Sekot had produced strange [[Sekotan Personal Starship|organic ships]] which could travel faster than most other starships in the galaxy. The [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] was determined to investigate the origins of these vessels and were also seeking the whereabouts of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Vergere]], who had gone missing while on a mission to the planet a year before. [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] were dispatched to investigate.

At the same time, a task force led by [[Raith Sienar/Legends|Raith Sienar]] and [[Blood Carver]] [[Ke Daiv]] were also dispatched to the planet at the compulsory behest of [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], then a political leader of [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]] and commander of the [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|Republic Outland Regions Security Force]]. Tarkin sought, in his seizure of the mysterious "living starship"-producing world, political and economic advantages via recognition by the galaxy's [[Palpatine/Legends|rising powers]].

When Obi-Wan Kenobi and his Padawan arrived at the planet, they succeeded in getting an organic starship crafted, which they named ''[[Jabitha]]''. They also discovered that Vergere had been taken by a mysterious alien species from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] known as the Far Outsiders—actually the extragalactic [[Yuuzhan Vong]].

Meanwhile, Raith Sienar's squadron arrived and dispatched Ke Daiv to secure a highly coveted Sekotan starship. Unfortunately, Daiv was rejected by the [[seed-partner]]s, the necessary [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] basis for the starship's construction. Soon after the news of this rejection had been received, Tarkin's forces converged upon Sienar's, as both the Security Force commander and the famous ship designer, mistrusting one another's motives, had double-crossed the other, which ultimately triggered detection of their presence by Sekot herself. Together they felt compelled, then, to unleash their forces on the living world.<ref name="RP">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref>

==The battle==
Tarkin's fleet included two [[Trade Federation cargo hauler]]s and ten light cruisers which served as escorts for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]]. Swarms of [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Vulture droids]] and [[Sky mine|sky-mines]] were unleashed on the peaceful world and its settlers.

In response, the living world dispatched [[Zonama Sekotan fighter]]s, though they were outnumbered. During the battle, Anakin and [[Jabitha Hal]]—the Sekotan [[Magister]]'s daughter—were captured by Tarkin's forces, which intercepted the ''Jabitha''.

However, Obi-Wan and Anakin managed to escape in their seedship, though it was wounded by enemy fire. In the end, Zonama Sekot left the [[Gardaji sector/Legends|sector]] of battle and, using its massive [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] system, escaped into the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]]. The attacking forces were left severely crippled.<ref name="RP">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref>

==Aftermath==
Obi-Wan and Anakin flew the ''Jabitha'' to the planet [[Seline]], where the living ship died from her wounds. In fact, ''all'' Sekotan ships throughout the galaxy, as a result of losing contact with Zonama Sekot, also died.

Kenobi and Skywalker's next mission was to the planet [[Kodai]], in which they sought to recover the ancient [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Holocron of King Adas|holocron]] of [[Adas|King Adas]].<ref name="RP">''[[Rogue Planet]]''</ref>
